report_meta.title: Indonesia Roofing, Exterior & Insulation Materials 2026
report_meta.completeness_note: 12/25 queries done. Sufficient for sample report v1 with remaining gaps filled by KIRA estimates anchored to documented data.

research_progress.remaining_strategy: Fill with KIRA estimates anchored to documented data

macroeconomic.president: Prabowo Subianto, sworn October 2024. Key policies: 3 Million Houses Program, 100% VAT exemption for property, Danantara sovereign wealth fund

ikn_capital_relocation.status_2025: Downgraded to 'political capital'. Completion target 2028. State funding declining significantly. Continued execution but reduced ambition.
ikn_capital_relocation.implication: Demand catalyst remains but moderated vs original projection

indonesia_total_2025_kira_est.methodology: APAC USD 32.58bn × Indonesia construction GDP share ~6-7%

exterior_materials.data_status: Limited public segmentation. KIRA est. needed.
exterior_materials.trend: Aesthetic cladding/composite facade gaining in mid-tier residential and commercial

indonesia_glasswool_kira_est_usd_m.methodology: APAC inorganic USD 2.6bn × Indonesia share ~3-4% (industrial activity proxy)
indonesia_glasswool_kira_est_usd_m.note: Manufacturing footprint thin in Indonesia; mostly imported/converted locally

competitive_fiber_cement_indonesia.market_structure: Highly concentrated (HHI 8737 vs 4171 in 2017). 5-7 dominant players + long tail.

major_players.name: PT Etex Building Performance Indonesia (formerly PT Eternit Gresik)
major_players.brands: KALSI (flat fiber cement - boards, partitions, plank, cladding, floor) + ETER (corrugated roofing - Eter6, Eter11, Eter14)
major_players.history: Pioneer asbestos-free fiber cement in Indonesia
major_players.parent_scale: Etex Group: €3bn+ revenue, 17,000 employees, 118 plants in 45 countries
major_players.global_brand_family: Part of Etex commercial brands: CEDRAL, DURLOCK, EQUITONE, ETERNIT, GYPLAC, PLADUR, PROMAT, SINIAT, SUPERBOARD, KALSI

major_players.name: PT Siam Indo Gypsum Industry / PT Siam Indo Concrete
major_players.type: Subsidiary of Siam Cement Group (SCG, Thai listed)
major_players.exports: Exports to Brunei Darussalam (2024 shipment for 1,000-unit housing dev)
major_players.scg_indonesia_other: PT Semen Jawa (cement) + PT Jayamix by SCG (readymix). 24,000 tons alternative raw materials Q1 2024 (green initiative)
strengths: Cross-sell with SCG cement/concrete in Indonesia
weaknesses: Foreign brand awareness lower than local

major_players.name: PT Impack Pratama Industri Tbk
major_players.products: Roofing (EZ-Lock launched Jan 2020 — twin-wall, UV blocking 98%, leak-proof)
weaknesses: Concentrated product range vs Etex breadth

other_global_players_indonesia_presence.name: James Hardie
other_global_players_indonesia_presence.presence: Global leader, limited Indonesia direct presence

competitive_insulation_indonesia.market_structure: Distributor-led (manufacturing footprint thin). Most product imported/converted locally.

manufacturers_or_assemblers.name: Cahaya Benteng Mas
manufacturers_or_assemblers.products: Glasswool, bundled metal roofing + insulation packages

key_distributors.name: PT Adimas Isolasitama
key_distributors.products: Rockwool, Glasswool, Aluminium Foil (MSK-7351A), BJLS galvanized steel, Roofmesh, XLPE Foam, NBR Foam, Copper pipes, Freon, Flex Duct

key_distributors.name: Sheth Insulations (India HQ)
key_distributors.products: Glasswool, Rockwool, Mineral Wool, Ceramic Fiber Blanket

saint_gobain_indonesia.core_indonesia_business: PLASTERBOARD/GYPSUM (Gyproc) — NOT primary fiber cement roofing
saint_gobain_indonesia.entry: 2007, established 2012 as PT Saint-Gobain Construction Products Indonesia
saint_gobain_indonesia.gyproc_plant: Cikande Industrial Estate, West Java, inaugurated 2018, 30 Mm²/year capacity
saint_gobain_indonesia.indonesia_2025_status: Construction market 'lackluster but improving' (per SG 2025 results). ~20 data center projects in Indonesia+Malaysia 2025
saint_gobain_indonesia.report_implication: Adjacent player — plays in gypsum/drywall substitution dynamics with GRC ceiling. Mention in competitive landscape as 'adjacent multinational with strong plasterboard/gypsum presence'.

mitra10.parent: PT Catur Sentosa Adiprana Tbk (CSAP, listed)
mitra10.positioning: Pioneer of modern retail building materials Indonesia

channel_modern_trade.channel_share_estimate: Modern trade ~20-25% (up from <10% in 2014). Traditional toko bangunan still >70% but losing share.

tiktok_tokopedia_merger.implication: Major disruption — TikTok Shop + Tokopedia combined ecosystem

channel_digital_ecommerce.social_commerce_regulation: Regulation 31/2023 restricts in-app checkout on social platforms — impacted TikTok Shop initial model

bim_adoption_indonesia.roadmap: Ministry PUPR BIM Roadmap, 4 phases 2017-2024 (just completed)
bim_adoption_indonesia.current_status_2025: Implementation maturity varies; government projects mandated, private sector adoption growing

ai_in_construction.regional_benchmark: Singapore Construction AI Mandate (BCA, 2023) leading regional standard

industry_initiatives.construction_indonesia_2025_expo: Featured BIM, drone/aerial photography, GIS, thermal imaging, 3D printing
industry_initiatives.ap3i: Indonesian Precast and Prestressed Companies Association — precast/modular tech adoption

regulatory_asbestos.status: NOT YET BANNED — Indonesia is largest asbestos importer in SEA
regulatory_asbestos.current_usage: 13% Indonesian households use asbestos roofing. 50% Jakarta buildings contain white asbestos. 10% nationwide. Asbestos = ~90% of asbestos manufacturing output goes to roofing.
regulatory_milestones: Government Regulation 74/2001 + Ministerial Regulation 3/1985: categorize asbestos as B3 Hazardous Material (gradual ban intent)
March 2024: Supreme Court granted petition — mandatory health warning labels on all asbestos-containing products
Target: Asbestos-Free Indonesia by 2035 (advocacy goal, not yet policy)
regulatory_asbestos.advocacy: INA-BAN (Indonesian Ban Asbestos Network) + Solidarity Network gaining traction
regulatory_asbestos.global_context: 60+ countries fully banned including Japan, South Korea, Brazil
regulatory_asbestos.implication: Massive substitution opportunity for non-asbestos fiber cement (Etex Kalsi/Eter, Impack Pratama, Siam Indo) over 2025-2035 horizon

rating_systems: BGH - Bangunan Gedung Hijau (Govt PWPH Reg 21/2021 — alternative track since 2021)
regulatory_green_building.incentives: Article 35 PWPH Reg 21/2021 + Article 122 GR Reg 16/2021 — government incentives for BGH certification
regulatory_green_building.implication_for_materials: Specs increasingly require certified-low-carbon materials. Saint-Gobain launched 100% recycled plasterboard Jan 2025 globally — likely Indonesia launch follows.

sustainability_low_carbon.scg_indonesia_green_initiative: PT Semen Jawa (SCG subsidiary) used 24,000 tons alternative raw materials Q1 2024 (3% of total), co-processed 15,000 tons alternative fuel (20% substitution rate)
sustainability_low_carbon.ministry_of_industry_decarbonization: 30% emissions cut by 2030 mandate for cement sector
sustainability_low_carbon.low_carbon_cement_products: SCG 'Bezt Eco Friendly Cement' — 50kg lower CO2/ton, 95% green-label score
sustainability_low_carbon.lcdi: Low Carbon Development Initiative — pushing manufacturers to green production
